$comment: Pinned Node.js runtime that Bunsen mounts into custom / non-bunsen container images so the platform tools (orchestrator, supervisor, scorers) have a Node interpreter inside the run container. SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for both packages/runtime/src/node-runtime.ts (the host-side resolver) and packages/agents/scripts/build-bundles.mjs (the from-source pre-fetch). These are glibc builds from nodejs.org; the sha256 values are anchored to the GPG-signed SHASUMS256.txt for the release (re-derivable via scripts: curl https://nodejs.org/dist/v<version>/SHASUMS256.txt). musl/Alpine container images are not yet supported (the platform Node closure is glibc; see docs/PLATFORM_TOOLS.md) — a musl variant would add entries keyed '<target>-musl' from unofficial-builds.nodejs.org. Bumping the version must stay in lockstep with the esbuild --target in build-bundles.mjs.
